adj. Not to be pushed out of its place; not to be put aside; firm, immovable; irrefragable; unanswerable.
E. adj. S. अ + स्था stand. Immoveable. See aṭal .

v. n. S. अट् wander, H. aiṅṭh a twist. 1. To walk affectedly or coquettishly; to express tenderness by gestures (itrānā) . 2. To stammer; mumble; mouth; drawl; clip one's words; lisp (tutlānā). Bachchoṅ kī tarah aṭhlā-ke mat bolo . 3. To give crooked answers (ṭeṛhā bolnā) . 4. To speak crossly, harshly, rudely. Maiṅ ne to sīdhe subhāo bāt pūchhī, woh yoṅhī aṭhlātī hai . 5. To pretend ignorance; to affect; not to know (maṭyānā , E.). Jān būjh-kar mat aṭhlāo . 6. To oppose; resist; contend with; to cross (one). Mujh se aiṅṭh lāyā, to sīdhā kar dūṅgā . W.
q. v.
s.m. Sunset; the west (cf. aṭhmanā).
s.m. The West.
= H اٿهماسا अठमासा aṭhmāsā, v.s.v. آٿهہ āṭh.
= H اتهماس अठमास aṭhmās, v.s.v. آٿهہ āṭh.

adv. S. याम् watch. Every hour of the day; day and night; always. Ab tum tajo garab, gumān, āṭhoṅ jām bhajo Bhagwān. Ab to kar le kar se kuchh dān, jis meṅ ho terā kalyān . Song. Khayāl . Now throw aside your pride, and praise God night and day, And with your hand, hand alms, salvation find you may. āṭhoṅ gāṅṭh kummaid, — — kummait , adj. 1. Skilful; clever; accomplished; gifted ( chātur ). 2. Shrewd; sagacious; needle-witted ( syānā ). 3. Expert; dexterous; adroit; smart; quick; ready ( tez ). 4. Cunning; nimble; fine-fingered; sharp ( chālāk ). 5. Conversant; versed; master of; cut out for; at home in ( ustād ). 6. Experienced; practised.
v.s.v. آٿهہ āṭh.
— v.s.v. آٿهہ āṭh.
v.s.v. آٿهہ āṭh.
q. v.

q. v.

def: adj. Eighth.
adj. S. अष्टमः Eighth. āṭh'wāṅ hissā . The eighth part.
adj. Eighth.
= اٿهماسا aṭhmāsā, q.v., s.v. آٿهہ āṭh. = H اتهماس अठमास aṭhmās, v.s.v. آٿهہ āṭh.
E. n. m. 1. The eighth day; eight days; a week. 2. The use of agricultural implements for a period of eight days. 3. A loan payable in full or by instalments at the end of the week.
s. m. The eighth day after any other; a week.
s. (dakh.) A week or eight days.
v.s.v. آٿهہ āṭh.
s.f. = اٿواٿی کهٿواٿي aṭwāṭī-khaṭwāṭī, q.v. s.f. Taking to one's bed in grief or trouble, &c.
more: s. f. The state of being confined to bed through sickness, &c.
subs: bag and baggage, household goods and chattels
or — lekar paṛnā, v.n. To take to one's bed, &c.

def: direct/oblique/vocative feminine singular/plural of آٹھواں (āṭhvā̃)
A constitutional amendment in Pakistan that grants the President the power to dismiss the Prime Minister and dissolve the National Assembly.; Ultimate authority; real power.
ex:
میرے ہی گھر میں موئی سوکن بنی بیٹھی تھی یہ
ناس جائے اس نگوڑی آٹھویں ترمیم کا۔

My own home has become a rival wife because of it
Damn that wretched Eighth Amendment.
– Inayat Ali Khan

ety: Pehl. ātāsh, ātars; Z. ātar; cf. S. अथरि
def: s.f. Fire, flame; anger, passion, rage
more: n. f. Ped. for H. āg, āṅch , Z. ātars . Fire; anger; passion; rage. ātish kā parkālā , H. āg kā putlā , n. 1. Sparks; scintillation; flames. Gusse meṅ ātish kā parkālā ban gayā . 2. A very sharp, knowing, wicked person an incendiary; fire brand. Ātish ke parkāle ko kaun chheṛ-kar balā le ? Who would bring evil on his head by provoking this firebrand? 3. One who is too full of energy and activity. 4. A very beautiful person; a brilliant dancer; a great coquette.
subs: adj. & s.m.f. Kindling fire; mischief-making, seditious, factious; — fuel; mischief-maker, firebrand, incendiary
adj. Casting, or casting out, fire
adj. & s.m.f. Kindling fire, spreading fire, stirring up fire, &c. ( = ātish-afroz, q.v.)
(bār, rt. of bārīdan, 'to rain'), adj. & s.m. Raining fire; flaming; flery; — a fire-lock; tinder-box
(bāz, rt. of bāḵẖtan or of bāzīdan, 'to play'), s.m. A maker of fireworks, a pyrotechnist
s.m. Spark, a lighted coal; a beautiful person; an exciter of quarrels; — adj. Quarrelsome
s.m. A fire-worshipper, guebre, one of the sect of the Magi, a disciple of Zoroaster
s.m. Fire-temple; fire-place; a park of artillery ( = top-ḵẖāna)
s.m. Fire-eater; the bartavella or Greek partridge ( = H. ćakor); a salamander
s.m. Any receptacle for fire, fire-place, grate, chafing-dish, brazier
adj. & s.m. Pouring out or scattering fire; — incendiary; fomenter of quarrels ( = ātish-afroz)
s.f. Conflagration; setting fire to houses (whether accidentally or by design), arson
and -zana, s.m. Striking fire; that which strikes fire; tinder; touchwood; anything combustible; a tinder-box with the flint and steel
s.f. The act of striking fire; also = ātish-zadagī
s.f. Region of fire; a place where fire abounds; a hot cannonade or musketry (in battle); a large fire; a funeral pile
adj. & s.m. Scattering fire; — a volcano
s.m. Spark, flame, scintillation; a firebrand, an incendiary; a very astute and wicked person; a very active and energetic person; a very beautiful person
s.m. Furnace; grate; chimney; fire-worshippers' temple
adj. Of a fiery disposition, hot-tempered, passionate, &c. ( = ātish-t̤abʻ)

def: adj. Thirsting, thirsty.

s. m. A guest, pilgrim, stranger, person entitled to the rights of hospitality.

r: ātithya, ātitheya, ātithya karnā, kā, ātithya-kārī
def: s.m. (f. -inī), A hospitable person; a host.
adj. (f. -ā), Sharp, hot acrid; very pungent.
adj. & adv. Much, very much; very, exceedingly; indeed.
s.m. A rigid fast observed on certain occasions.

s.m. An antidote to poison.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Not sharp, dull, stupid; — a stupid person, &c.

s.f. Small thatched room on the roof of a house; cottage; hut.

def: s.f. Twist, twine, turn, flexure, fold, ply, plait; bundle, hank or skein of thread; sheaf; handful of stalks of grain; a turban (a cant term)

def: s.m. (f. -ā), A great talker.
s.f. Superfluous talk; garrulity; exaggeration.
s.m. The earthen vessel in which victuals are dressed for the public at the door of the temple of Jagannāth.
more: 1. s. m. The pot in which victuals are dressed for the publick at the door of the temple of Jagannāth. 2. part. past. (of اٿکنا) Aground, stopped, hindered.

def: part. adj. Stopped, hindered; detained; entangled, &c.; stuck; aground.
s.f. Guess, conjecture, supposition; rough computation or estimate; skill in judging, discernment, judgment, opinion; quantity; size
more: s. f. Guess, conjecture, judgment, opinion, quantity, size. اٿکل باز aṭkal-bāz, adj. Adroit at guessing; an appraiser.
subs: (aṭkal+S. paća+uka), adj. & s.m. Random, uncertain; — random guess, mere conjecture; — adv. By guess, at random
To recite the first or opening chapter of the Qorʼān for a deceased person whose name is unknown; to make a pretence of reading the Qorʼān; to use earnest importunity for what is impossible to be had
adv. By guess; approximately; at random
and aṭkan-maṭkan or aṭkan-baṭkan, s.m. A child's game, something like our "guess if you can."

def: n. f. S. अट् to wander, कल to reckon. 1. Discernment; judgment; skill ( āṅk ). Ise kapṛe kī baṛī aṭkal hai . He is a good judge of cloth. 2. Guess; rough estimate; conjecture. Phr. To give a guess. aṭ'kal-bāz , adj. One who makes a good guess.

n. m. A wild guess; a random guess; a mere conjecture; guesswork. Aṭkal-pachchū, gair muqarrar . W. Prov. = Uṭakkar pache deṛh sau . E. Prov. All guess and no certainty. aṭkal pachchū, uṭakkarlais , adj. 1. Uncertain; not exact; conjectural; without thought or premeditation. 2. Nonsensical; unmeaning; absurd; foolish. Soch-kar kaho, utakkarlais jawāb na do . Think before you speak, and don't talk nonsense. aṭkal se , adv. By guess; approximately; at random. aṭ'kal pach' chū kī fātiha paṛhnā , v. a. 1. To repeat a certain portion of the Korān as a prayer for a deceased person whose name is unknown. 2. To make a pretence of reading the Korān.
adj. Conjectural; probable; approximate.
adj. Conjectural; probable; presumptive.
v.t. To guess, conjecture; to judge, think; to estimate, value, appraise; to make a rough estimate; to find out; to see through (one)
more: (see اٿکل) v. a. To guess, to conjecture, to judge, to estimate, to think.

def: v. a. 1. To guess; to make a rough guess; conjecture. 2. To estimate; make a rough estimate; value; assess ( āṅknā ). 3. To find out; to see through one; to take one's measure; to make out.
s. m. One who guesses at a venture, or without grounds on which to judge. adv. At random.

r: aṭ'kan maṭ'kan, aṭ'kal maṭ'kal, E. aṭ'kan baṭ'kan
def: W. n. m. A child's game. Aṭkan batkan, dahī chaṭākan; Aglā jhūle baglā jhūle; Sāwan mās karelā phūle . Children's game. A pot of rice, lick up the tyre; See swinging here, and swinging there; In August rains Karelā flowers.

v. a. 1. To hinder; interrupt; stop; hold back; impede; retard; hamper; obstruct; thwart; cut off; prevent; bar; clog; check. Phr. To stand in the way of; stop the way. 1. Chaltī gāṛī meṅ roṛā aṭkānā . To put a spoke in the wheel. 2. Pānī ne aṭkā rakkhā hai . The rain detains (me). 2. To confine; keep back; restrain; intercept; detain. 1. Rupaye ne iskī jān aṭkā rakkhī hai . His money detains his soul. 2. Ustād ne ek-hī kitāb meṅ aṭkā rakkhā hai . The master has kept me back in just one book. 3. To couple; attach; append; brace; join together; bracket. Bail ko jūe meṅ aṭkā do . Yoke the oxen. 4. To chain; fasten; hook; rivet. Kāṅṭe se kāṅṭā aṭkā lo . 5. To pin; stitch; to put in a few stitches ( ṭāṅkā lagānā ). Ab to zarā aṭkā do, phir pakkā ho jāëgā . Just stitch it for the present, it will be sewn afterwards. 6. To lock; lock up. 7. To betroth; to join in wedlock; to marry. Ab apne chhoṭe beṭe ko bhī kahīṅ aṭkā do . Now marry your younger son also to somebody. 8. To retrench; put under stoppages; withhold; keep back. Mȳāṅ, kyoṅ aṭkā rakkhā hai? De dilā ke pāp kāṭo . Sir, why do you keep back (my money). Give it and wipe off your sins. 9. To suspend; postpone; put off. Is muqadme ko aṭkā rakkho . 10. To wear; put on; slip on. Ab to isī ko aṭkā lo, phir dūsrā pahin lenā . Put on this for the present, you can wear another next time. aṭkāo meṅ . adv. Under control, restraint, etc.
v.t. To obstruct, hinder, impede, interrupt, stop, bar, prevent, check, restrain, keep back, detain; to fasten, join, attach, unite (in marriage); to put on, wear
more: (caus. of اٿکنا) v. a. To stop, to prevent, to hinder, restrain, detain, coerce.
subs: v.t. To keep back, withhold, detain; to put off, postpone

def: s. m. Stop, prevention, hinderance, restraint, detention.
s.m. Hindrance, prevention, &c. ( = اٿك aṭak, q.v.)
subs: Under control or restraint; under arrest; fettered, clogged, &c.

def: Same as اٿکاؤ aṭkāʼo, q. v.
(Prov.) = اٿکل aṭkal, q.v. s.f. Guess, conjecture, supposition; rough computation or estimate; skill in judging, discernment, judgment, opinion; quantity; size
subs: (aṭkal+S. paća+uka), adj. & s.m. Random, uncertain; — random guess, mere conjecture; — adv. By guess, at random
To recite the first or opening chapter of the Qorʼān for a deceased person whose name is unknown; to make a pretence of reading the Qorʼān; to use earnest importunity for what is impossible to be had
adv. By guess; approximately; at random
and aṭkan-maṭkan or aṭkan-baṭkan, s.m. A child's game, something like our "guess if you can."

def: s.m. (f. -inī), A caste of silk-weavers in the Deccan (dakkhan).
s.m. = اٿکاو aṭkāʼo, q.v. s.m. Hindrance, prevention, &c. ( = اٿك aṭak, q.v.)
subs: Under control or restraint; under arrest; fettered, clogged, &c.

def: adj. Playful, sportive, lively, gay, merry; frivolous, light, coquettish, wanton.

adj. S. अट्ट much, खेल to move to and fro. 1. Playful; sportive; airy; sprightly; gay; lively; merry; lightsome; blithe; winsome; jovial; jolly. 2. Light; skittish; coquettish; wanton. aṭ-khe'lī, aṭkhel-panā,—pan , n. 1. Playfulness; sportiveness; sprightliness. 2. Mirth; merriment; merry-making; gaiety. 3. Coquettishness; an affected or mincing gait; graceful motion. Būṭā se qad, aur is tere aṭkhel-pane par, Ho kyoṅ na dugānāh * tere qurbān, dugānā? Wom. Raṅgīn. An elfin small, and such your sportive grace, How not to you, my soul, devoted be? 4. Friskiness; skittishness; amorous glances or gestures. aṭhkhelyāṅ, aṭhkelyoṅ kī chāl , n. f. 1. Sport; play; romp; sportive mirth. Bachchoṅ kī aṭhkhelyāṅ jī lubhātī haiṅ . The sportive mirth of children captivates the heart. 2. An affected or coquettish gait; coquettish airs; a mincing step. Chāl aṭkhelyoṅ se chaltī āī. Díl ko pāoṅ ke nīche maltī āī . Shauq. With a coquettish, wanton grace she moved, Trampling beneath her feet all hearts, she moved. aṭhkhelyoṅ se chalnā , v. n. To walk coquettishly, as a wanton, etc. Bach-pan meṅ sab aṭhkhelyoṅ se chalte haiṅ .
s.f. Playful, sportive, &c. girl or woman.
adj. = aṭkhel; and s.f. = aṭkhelpan, qq.v. adj. Playful, sportive, lively, gay, merry; frivolous, light, coquettish, wanton.
more: s. f. Wantonness, an affected (also a graceful) pace or mode of walking.

def: s.m. Playfulness, sportiveness, gambol, mirth, merriment; coquettishness, wantonness; affected or mincing gait; graceful motion.
s.m. Playfulness, sportiveness, gambol, mirth, merriment; coquettishness, wantonness; affected or mincing gait; graceful motion.
adj. & s.m. Pious, devout, righteous, virtuous, just; — religious men, devotees; the abstemious; the pious; &c.
more: s. plur. (of تقي) Religious men, devotees.

ety: S. अति+v.n. of rt. लड् = लल्
def: s.m. Darling, pet, spoilt child ( = lāḍlā or lāṛlā).
W. n. m. Darling; spoilt child; enfant gāte .
s.m. f. (?), Satin.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اطلس (atlas), from Arabic أَطْلَس (ʔaṭlas).

def: adj. Of satin; made of satin; like satin.
s.m. The Supreme Spirit
more: n. m. See. aṭālā. Aṭam hai khāk kā, yā rākh kā ḍher. Kaheṅ haiṅ is ko hāthī, hai ye andher . Sauda. It is a pile of dust, or say, an ash-heap 'tis, They say it is an elephant, how strange it is.
s. m. Soul, self. (much used in the composition of Hindawī words; as) آتم پالک ātm-pālak, Selfish; آتم گهات ātm-ghāt, Self-murder; آتم نامي ātm-nāmī, Self-evident.
subs: s.m. The all-pervading spirit of Rāma
(-kī), To mortify the body; to cause pain (to), to afflict, inflict pain (on)
s.f. Self-knowledge; the knowledge of spirit
s.m. Knowledge of the mind of soul; self-knowledge; self-consciousness
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Self-conceited, proud, haughty; selfish; — a coxcomb, &c.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Self-cherishing; — selfish person; self-supporting person
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Born from oneself; — one's own offspring; child, son
s.m. (f. -ā), One who knows self; one who knows the Suprome Spirit
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Knowing spirit or self; — a mental philosopher; a psychologist
s.m. (f. -ī), and ātma-devatā, s.f. Tutelar deity
s.m. One's own pleasure or happiness
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Restraining self; — one who practises self-restraint; a stoical person
s.m. One's own private affairs (see āp-kāj)
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), and ātma-ghātī, adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Committing suicide; suicidal; a suicide, self-destroyer
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Speaking of self, or of soul; a mental philosopher, psychologist
s.f. The science of the knowledge of mind, or soul, or self
adj. Appertaining to self or to spirit

def: s.m. The Supreme Spirit
more: n. f. S. त्मन् vital breath. 1. Spirit; soul; mind; self, 2. The inner man; the ego. Ham kyā, hamārī ātmā asīs degī . 3. Appetite; hunger; appetence ( bhūk ). Ṭukṛā khāyā, to ātmā meṅ ṭhanḍak paṛī . 4. Belly; stomach; abdomen ( peṭ ). Ātmā meṅ paṛe, to Parmātmā kī sūjhe . Prov. When the belly is full you may see the Supreme soul. ātmā param-ātmā , n. m. The supreme soul; the Supreme God. ātmā ṭhan' ḍī honā . v. a. To be pleased, satisfied, consoled, gratified. ātmā kalpānā,—satānā , v. a. To cause or inflict pain; mortify; hurt. Phr. To wound one's feelings; to break the heart; to wring the heart. Kyoṅ kisī rānḍ bevā kī ātmā kalpāte ho?
s. f. The soul, the body, the self, the mind, the understanding. (Hence the Greek Αυτμη ).
subs: s.m. The all-pervading spirit of Rāma
(-kī), To mortify the body; to cause pain (to), to afflict, inflict pain (on)
s.f. Self-knowledge; the knowledge of spirit
s.m. Knowledge of the mind of soul; self-knowledge; self-consciousness
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Self-conceited, proud, haughty; selfish; — a coxcomb, &c.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Self-cherishing; — selfish person; self-supporting person
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Born from oneself; — one's own offspring; child, son
s.m. (f. -ā), One who knows self; one who knows the Suprome Spirit
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Knowing spirit or self; — a mental philosopher; a psychologist
s.m. (f. -ī), and ātma-devatā, s.f. Tutelar deity
s.m. One's own pleasure or happiness
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Restraining self; — one who practises self-restraint; a stoical person
s.m. One's own private affairs (see āp-kāj)
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), and ātma-ghātī, adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Committing suicide; suicidal; a suicide, self-destroyer
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Speaking of self, or of soul; a mental philosopher, psychologist
s.f. The science of the knowledge of mind, or soul, or self
adj. Appertaining to self or to spirit

def: for compounds of which ātma is the first member, v.s.v. آتما ātmā.
s. m. A son.
bread, naan
n. Swallow wort; Asclepias asthmatica . Wat.
s. f. The soul: see آتما ātmā. آنا آتمان ānā ātmāṅ (for अनात्मा ) The reverse of soul.
adj. Appertaining to the spiritual; belonging to self.
s.f. Identity, sameness.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Appertaining to oneself, own, special, peculiar; related, cognate, akin; — friend; kinsman; congener.

ety: S. आवृत, rt. वृ; cf. H. āṭnā
def: v.n. To be held or contained; to go or fit (into, -meṅ); to be filled up, be full.
more: v. n. 1. To be contained. 2. To be filled. 3. To wander, to perambulate, to walk about. کوا اٿ گیا kūā aṭ gayā, The well is filled up.

(from ٿني Heb. He turned, bent, doubled, &c.) s. m. The middle, interstice, interval: folds or plaits. اٿنائي راه as̤nā-i-rāh, or اٿناي راه مین as̤nā-i-rāh meṅ, In the middle of the road, whilst travelling.

def: v.t. = اٿانا aṭānā, and v.n. = اٿنا aṭnā, qq.v. v.t. To fix (a thing, into, -meṅ); to fill, get (a thing, into); to fasten (on, -meṅ), to join (to, -meṅ).
s.f. pl. (of طنب t̤unub), Tent-ropes.
= آٿهون āṭhoṅ, q.v. v.s.v. آٿهہ āṭh.
s.m. Nature, constitution, essence; power, control; dexterity, skill.
adj. & adv. Without let or hindrance, unrestrained, uninterrupted, unquestioned, unchallenged; unfailing, unbroken; — uninterruptedly, continuously; smoothly. (The more com. form is بےٿوك be ṭok.)
more: adj. Unrestrained, uninterrupted, without hinderance, unquestioned.

def: adj. Rare, for be-ṭok . 1. Unrestrained; uninterrupted; unchecked; without let or hindrance; unquestioned. 2. Unbroken; unintermitting; never-failing. —adv. smoothly; at a stretch; continuously.
adj. = اتل atul, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Not weighed; not weighable; immeasurable; incalculable; unequalled; unequal; unparalleled.
adj. (f. -ā), Rough, unpolished (as stones, jewels, &c.); uncouth, rude, unmannerly (person).
more: adj. Unpolished (stones or jewels; and, hence, of persons), rude, unmannerly.

def: = اٿپٿي aṭpaṭī, q.v. s.f. Nonsensical talk; confused or incoherent speech; crooked answer; prevarication, evasion.
v.n. To be agitated, or restless, or confused; to be tired; to be out of sorts; to vacillate, waver, hesitate; to linger, delay; to speak incoherently, or at random; to talk nonsense
subs: To speak at random; to talk nonsense.

def: v. n. S. अट् wander, पत् fall. To hesitate; linger; delay; keep back. Sab log aṭpaṭāë pāchhe rahe . Rājnīti. aṭ paṭ bol'nā , E. v. n. To speak at random; to talk nonsense. Laṭ paṭ pag dhartī dhare, aṭ paṭ bolat bain . Sabhā bilās. With wanton gait she treads the ground, and prates (sweet) nonsense (with her lips).
adj. (f. -ā), Poor, miserable, wretched; wicked; disorderly, irregular; thoughtless, absurd.
s.f. Poverty, wretchedness; wickedness; absurdity, nonsense.
s.f. Nonsensical talk; confused or incoherent speech; crooked answer; prevarication, evasion.

n. f. 1. Nonsensical talk; crooked answers. 2. Prevarication; evasion; equivocation. āṭam pāṭam , adv. Hither and thither; here and there; up and down; all about; every where. Tab gūṅje, tab paṛe bajār. Āṭam pāṭam phire dalāl . E. Song. Chak Chandā . When hunger gnạws, to market (for release); (His stomach filled) the touter strolls at ease.
s.f. Nonsensical talk; confused or incoherent speech; crooked answer; prevarication, evasion.
adv. Here, in this place.
more: n. m. S. उत् high? 1. An answer; reply; rejoinder; defence. 2. The north.
s. m. An answer, the north. adj. Posterior, latter, northern, superiour, high. उत्तरार्ध uttarārdh, The latter half.

def: adj. Limiting, extreme
more: s. f. plur. (of طرف) Sides, environs, confines, skirts, outparts, districts.
subs: s.f. Environs of a city or town, suburbs.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَطْرَاف (atrāf), from Arabic أَطْرَاف (ʔaṭrāf).

def: adj. Extreme, outer, outlying, limitary, limiting, &c.
subs: s.m. A pass for the transit of dutiable goods beyond the limits of the custom stations.

def: s.m. Tartars; Tartar, Turcoman.
s.f. = انتڙي aṅtṛi, q.v.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Unsatisfied; restless, uneasy.
s.f. Inability to be satisfied, insatiableness.

ety: for A. عطسة, n. of un. fr. ʻat̤s, inf. n. of عطس 'to sneeze'
def: s.m. Sneezing, a sneeze.

S. अड्ड join, सद् adhere; A jingling reduplication, n. f. 1. Knot; alliance; league; confederacy (sāzish) . 2. Attachment; friendship; affection (āshnāī) . Is ranḍī bhaṛve meṅ muddat se atsaṭ hai . There has been a long-standing liason between this harlot and the pimp. 3. Work; business; stroke of policy. Ghar kī aṭsaṭ meṅ, sārā din batīt hotā hai . Wom. The whole day goes in household work. 4. Skill; cleverness; head piece; ingenuity; shrewdness; hocus pocus (Slang); dexterity; adroitness; tact; knowledge of the world. Aṭsaṭ se chār paise kamā letā hai . By his adroitness he contrives to earn four pice a day. 5. Anxiety; care; solicitude. Rahe mudām sarokār aish se us ko! Na hone pāve kisī taur kī use aṭsaṭ . Inshā. May pleasure always with him be, Nor care t'assail him suffered be. aṭsaṭ ho'nā,—laṛ'nā , v. a. 1. To be linked to, entangled, etc. 2. To form a league, or confederacy; to enter into an alliance; to be combined. Sāre amle kī aṭsaṭ laṛī huī hai . All the ministerial officers are in league. 3. To fall in love; to be hooked (Slang) ; to be hand and glove with. Is se us se aṭ saṭ ho gaī . These two are entangled with one another.

s.f. Close union, alliance, league, confederacy; intimacy, close attachment, liaison, entanglement, amour; necessary or inseparable work or business (of a house); care, solicitude; shrewdness, astuteness; dexterity, skill, ingenuity, adroitness, tact
more: adj. (dakh.) Sixty eight: see اٿهسٿهہ aṭhsaṭh.
subs: (-se), To be attached (to); to be entangled (with); to be in league (with), &c.

def: adj. Thirsty; very thirsty (after, or for); desiring, longing, burning with desire (for).
adj. Thirsty; very thirsty (after, or for); desiring, longing, burning with desire (for).
s.f. Flax, Linun usitatissimum (cf. tīsī and alsī.)
E. n. See alsī .
s.m. Heap, pile, store.
s.m. Loud laughter; horse-laugh.
s.f. A house of two or more stories; a high house; a palace.
s.m. (orig.) 'A seller of perfumes,' a perfumer; (in Urdū) a druggist, an apothecary.
more: n. m. عطر perfume. lit . a perfumer. A druggist; an apothecary. Attār kā shīsha aur madārī kā piṭārā . Prov. The druggist's bottle and the juggler's magic case. (The one professes to heal every disease, and the other can work all sorts of wonders.)
(adj. superl. of عطر) s. m. A perfumer, a druggist.

def: adj. Of or relating to a druggist; — s.f. The business of a druggist.
more: n. f. The business of a druggist.
s. f. The business of a perfumer or druggist.

def: = آتتائي ātatāyī, q.v. s.m. (f. -inī). = H اتتاهي अतत्ताही atattāhī, s.m. Felon; thief; incendiary; ravisher; murderer; one who does any grievous wrong
subs: To commit a grievous wrong; to spoil, rob, &c.; to transgress, exceed bounds; to exaggerate.

def: s.m. Aggregate of eight (e.g. tīn aṭṭhe ćaubīs); the eight of a suit of cards; throw of eight with three dice.
more: (dakh.) 1. s. m. Throng ( Harris ), concourse. 2. adj. Eight: see آٿهہ āṭh.

def: n. m. The eight at cards.
adj. S. अष्टाविंशतिः Twenty-eight.
adj. Ninety-eight.
adj. S. अष्टानवतिः Ninety-eight.
adj. Eighteen. aṭṭhārah kaṅka'rā—goṭī , n. m. A game played with 18 pebbles; a kind of draughts.
adj. Eighteen
more: adj. Eighteen. اٿهاره بهار aṭhārah bhār, Various, of different sorts.
subs: m. pl. A body of lazy disorderly fellows, crew of idlers, pack of knaves
— goṭī, s.m. A certain game played with eighteen pebbles (something like our draughts).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀝𑁆𑀞𑀸𑀭𑀲 (aṭṭhārasa), from Sanskrit अष्टादश (aṣṭā́daśa).

def: adj. Eighteenth.
= اٿهاره وان aṭhārahwāṅ, q.v. adj. Eighteenth.
adj. Fifty-eight.
adj. S. अष्टापंचाशत्, Fifty-eight.

n. f. 1. A skein or hank of thread. 2. A twist; fold; plait; flexure. 3. A sheaf; a handful of stalks of grain given to the reapers for cutting a bojhā , or load, usually about a sixteenth part. 4. A cant word for a turban. āṭī jhoṭī , n. f. Twisting; folding together; contortion.
s.f. Twist, twine, turn, flexure, fold, ply, plait; bundle, hank or skein of thread; sheaf; handful of stalks of grain; a turban (a cant term)
more: s. f. A hank or bundle of thread; a cant word for a turban.
subs: s.f. Complication, twisting, folding together; contortion.

def: s.f. = آتون ātūn, q.v. s.f. Female teacher, preceptress, governess.
more: n. f. A female teacher; a governess. Ātū jī, ādāb . Wom.
s. f. A female teacher or a governess.

def: adj. S. atulyā , Pr. atulam unequalled, S. तुल to weigh. Unweighed; that cannot be weighed; immeasureable; incalculable. 1. Pāṅḍwan kī lakho lachhmī, jāno atul anūp . 2. An-tule bojh tale baiṭhā, sāṅchī sāṅchī bol . Rus. Now mind, you are sitting below an untold weight (the roof); so you had better speak the truth.
more: adj. S. चर्, चल, H. टल to move. 1. Fixed; immoveable; unmoved; rooted; fast; stationary; unable to stir. Phr. At a stand-still; at a stand. 2. Unshaken; unwavering; bent upon; set upon; unswerving; unflinching. Phr. Through thick and thin; coute qui coute ; game to the backbone. (Slang) . 3. Steadfast; staunch; persistent; persevering; undeviating. Woh apne irāde meṅ aṭal hai . 4. Proof against; steeled; firm; unbending; unyielding; inflexible; decided; resolved; resolute; strong; determined. Woh baṛā aṭal hai, kisī se muṅh nahīṅ phertā . 5. Stubborn; dogged; headstrong; inexorable. Phr. Per fas et nefas; foṛtiteṛ in re . Paṛe haiṅge aṭal ho, phūle phāle arne bhaiṅse se . Saudā. Their bellies are so full that they lie, like wild buffaloes, unable to move. 6. Unchangeable; immutable; constant; invariable; indefeasable; indissoluble; indelible; irreversible; inevitable; irrevocable; insusc…

def: adj. (f. -ā), Not weighed; not weighable; immeasurable; incalculable; unequalled; unequal; unparalleled.
more: adj. Unweighed, that cannot be weighed.

def: part. (f. -ā), Not weighed; increased, risen, swollen.
adj. (f. -ā), Not weighed; not weighable; immeasurable; incalculable; unequalled; unequal; unparalleled.
s.f. Female teacher, preceptress, governess.
more: ātūn-jī, s. f. (dakh.) A governant, a schoolmistress ( Harris ): see آتو ātū.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Not stout, thin, lean.

adj. (f. -ā), Hurt, wounded, grieved; affected, suffering, sick, diseased, distempered; impelled; agitated, restless, impatient; hasty, precipitate; quick, active, ready, prepared. (In these last senses the word would appear to be connected with the S. त्वरि.]
more: adj. S. त्वर् to hurry. 1. Hasty; precipitate. 2. Energetic; active; quick. 3. Ready; prepared; on foot. Rām kāj karve ko ātur . Brij. atur'tā, aturtā'ī , n. f. Haste; hurry; speed. Āp ne is kām meṅ aturtāī kyoṅ kī ? E.

def: s.f. Despatch, speed, activity, fleetness; haste, hurry. — aturtāʼī karnā (-meṅ), To act hastily (in), &c.
s.f. Despatch, speed, activity, fleetness; haste, hurry. — aturtāʼī karnā (-meṅ), To act hastily (in), &c.
adj. (f. -ā), Not liable to be broken, strong, indestructible; inexhaustible, abundant, unfailing, continuous, incessant.
more: adj. Not liable to be broken; inexhaustible.

def: adj. S. त्रुट् to tear. Not liable to be broken; inexhaustible.
adj. compar. & superl. (of طول t̤ūl), Longer; longest; very long; prolix.
more: adj. compar. (of طول) Longer, longest; prolix.

def: = آٿهوان āṭhwāṅ, q.v. adj. Eighth.
s.m. pl. (of طور t̤aur), Manners, modes, methods; ways, behaviour, practice, habits, conduct, deportment; dealings, mode of business.
more: s. m. plur. (of طور) Manners, modes, methods, ways, behaviour, practice, habits, devoirs. خوش اطوار ḵẖụsh-at̤wār, Well-mannered, well-bred.

def: To be confined to bed in great trouble or affliction.

r: aṭwā'ṭī khaṭwāṭī, khaṭwāṭī paṭwāṭī, ansan yā āsan pāṭī
def: n. f. 1. Taking to one's bed in the sulks. [It was the custom of a Rānī (queen), when her feelings were deeply wounded, to tie a bandage (paṭṭī) over her eyes and take to her bed, on which she lay perfectly still and silent.] 2. Bed and baggage; chattels; household goods. Apnī aṭwāṭī khaṭwāṭī le jā. — —lenā,— —le kar paṛ'nā , v. n. To take to one's bed in the sulks, or in trouble or affliction.
s.f. Taking to one's bed in grief or trouble, &c.
subs: bag and baggage, household goods and chattels
or — lekar paṛnā, v.n. To take to one's bed, &c.

ety: S. खटवा+स्थ, the first member being simply a jingling ryme; or otherwise aṭwāṭ = S. आर्ति+मत्
def: s.f. The state of a person who takes to his bed in grief or trouble, or feigning illness, or in the dumps or sulks. (See next.)
more: s. f. The state of a person who in great affliction remains bed-rid.

def: atrocity, excess, misdeed inflicted on someone
s.m. Deviation from prescribed observances; contemning religious and moral laws.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Transgressing religious rules; — transgression.
wety: From اَتْیَا چَار (atyā cār, “excess, atrocity”) + ـِی (-ī).

def: adj. & adv. Very little, very few.
adj. (f. -ā) & s.m. Very joyful; — excessive gladness or joy.
s.f. Excessive joy or gladness.
adj. & adv. Much, very much, exceeding, vast; — exceedingly, too
subs: adv. To the utmost, to the very last degree.

def: s.f. Superabundance, excessiveness.
adj. (f. -ā), Exorbitant, excessive, very dear.
adj. Very necessary, of essentialimportance.
s.m. Passing away, perishing; death; danger, risk; suffering.

ety: and also H. अत्युत्तम atyuttama, अति+उत्तम
def: adj. Exceedingly good, the very best; most excellent, super-excellent; rare.
conj. And = aur, q.v. adj. And, also, for the rest, besides, again, moreover; but, yet, still; over, else; and lo!; — another, other, different; more, additional
subs: Others, others besides; other kinds of
And again, and more, still more, also, moreover
Others apart, others being put aside
All other, all the others, the rest
One word more; — only hear! just listen!
Anyone else, any other, another, different ( = koʼī aur)
Anything else, some other, some or something more; something different ( = kućh aur)
What else? what more? what next? — certainly, assuredly, of course
And if not then; and otherwise
And this that; moreover, besides that.

def: s.m. The letter or sound au.

ety: of وبش, per met. for بوش, not in use
def: s.m. (orig.), A mixed company or crowd of people (especially of the meanest sort), mob; canaille; — a bad character, dissolute fellow, profligate, debauchee, rake, libertine; — adj. Dissolute, profligate, rakish, lecherous; — aubāsh honā, To become depraved or dissolute by low company.
more: n. m. pl. of وبش the common people. A rake; debauchee; profligate; a bad character. aubāsh' , adj. Lecherous; libidinous; profligate; dissolute.

s. m. 1. A rake, a dissolute fellow, debauchee, vagabond, libertine. 2. A mixed crowd of various nations and of all sorts of people, but especially of the meanest; the mob, the canaille. 3. adj. Debauched, dissolute, rakish.

def: adj. Like a rake; rakish, lissolute.
more: adv. or adj. Like a rake, rakish, dissolute.

def: s.f. Depravity, profligacy, dissipation, dissoluteness, debauchery, rakishness, lewdness; fondness for attending games and spectacles.
more: n. f. 1. Rakishness; dissoluteness; debauchery; lewdness. 2. Wenching; womanizing; incontinence; harlotry. 3. Depravity; profligacy.
s. f. Rakishness, dissoluteness, debauchery, dissipation, attending at games and spectacles.

def: W.; aukhā , Gaṛh. adj. au bad, bāṭ way, path. Uneven, bad, steep (road).
adj. Rough, rugged, uneven, impassable, impracticable (a road); steep, inaccessible, &c. ( = aughaṭ, q.v.)
more: (perhaps, from अव q. v. and वाट Way, or for اوگهٿ aughaṭ, q. v.) adj. Impassable, steep, inaccessible, devious.

def: adj. = aubaṭ, q.v. adj. Rough, rugged, uneven, impassable, impracticable (a road); steep, inaccessible, &c. ( = aughaṭ, q.v.)
adv. Unexpectedly, suddenly, unawares ( = aćānćak and aćānak, q.q.v.).
adv. Unexpectedly, suddenly, unawares ( = aćānćak and aćānak, q.q.v.).
s.m. The plant Morinda tinctoria (or M. citrifolia), from the root of which an orange colour is extracted ( = āć).
n. m. A root from which an orange color is extracted; Morinda citrifolia .
more: s. m. Name of a root from which an orange colour is extracted. (Morinda citrifolia).

def: s.m. Fitness, suitableness, aptness, propriety, rightness.
s.m. Returning; a return; a relapse; — a man advanced in years, a man of experience and wisdom, or of ability and prudence
subs: v.n. To return or revert (to), to fall back (upon); to have a relapse (of).

def: s.m. That which is thrown in or given over and above what is purchased, to boot, gratis.
more: (perhaps from अव and r. दा Give, or उदु and r. दा ) s. m. Gratis, what is given over and above or without purchase.

def: s.m. Generosity, liberality, munificence.
n. m. Defamation. See ajas .
s.f. Ill, evil, misfortune, calamity.
more: n. f. Misfortune; calamity ( biptā ). Kit bhauṅrā, kit ketkī, kit ambā, kit nīr, Joṅ joṅ paṛe hai audsā, woṅ woṅ sahe sarīr . Dohā. The beetle lives although the cactus flower be dead, The mango blossoms though no water be supplied, And thus the body bears all ills with which it's tried.

def: s.m. Loneliness, solitude.
adj. & s.m. White; grey; — white or grey colour (cf. ūdā).
s.m. Foreign country, strange land = pardes and udes, q.q.v. s.m. Quicksilver, mercury; — adj. Heavy, weighty
subs: pārā pilānā (meṅ, -ko), To fill or load with quicksilver; to make heavy

def: adj. (dakh.) Unfortunate, wretched (?).
s.m. Foreign country, strange land = pardes and udes, q.q.v. s.m. Quicksilver, mercury; — adj. Heavy, weighty
subs: pārā pilānā (meṅ, -ko), To fill or load with quicksilver; to make heavy

def: s.m. Foreigner, stranger = pardesī (the more com. term).

adj. = auṅdhā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), With the face down; upset, upside down, overturned, inverted, topsy-turvy, in utter confusion, ruined; adverse (as fortune); crooked, distorted; — s.m. Blockhead, fool
subs: v.n. To lie on the face
adj. Crooked-headed; ill-fated, unlucky, unfortunate; shortsighted
adv. On the face, with the face or head down; head-foremost, headlong
lit. 'To suck with the face down'; to act like a child, to be silly, ignorant, &c.

def: adj. (dakh.) Improper: see بيجا be-jā under بي be.
adj. (dakh.) Improper: see بيجا be-jā under بي be.
adj. Deep, unfathomable.
adj. Ill-formed, awkward, ungainly, uncouth, &c. ( = angaṛh, q.v.); — adv. Awkwardly, &c.
adj. H. au bad, ghaṛā formed. Awkward ( angaṛh , 6).
more: n. m. ( Ṭhagī ). A bad omen.

def: adj. Rough, uneven, impassable, impracticable (as a road), difficult of access, steep, inaccessible; unfrequented (cf. aubaṭ and ūbaṛ).

Māṛ.; aubaṭ , E. adj. H. au bad, ghāṭ pass, ferry. Rugged; rough; difficult; impassable. 1. Aughaṭ chale, na chaupaṭ gire . Prov. Who will bad roads essay upon their backs must lay. 2. Aughaṭ ghāṭ, bikaṭ kā paiṅḍā, pavan lagi tharrāta hai, Chātur chātur pār utar gaë, mūṛh khaṛā chillātā hai . Hymn. Rugged the bank, the bridge so scant, wind-struck it trembles so, The sharp ones all have crossed, the fool screams on the bank below.
s.f. A driving whip, a whip like a waggoner's (about seven cubits long, used in training horses); — an ornamental edging of superior country shoes (peculiar to Dehli); — a reel or skein.
more: n. f. 1. A whip, about seven cubits long, like a waggoner's, used in training horses. 2. An ornamental edging of superior country shoes (peculiar to Delhī). 3. A pit dug for catching elephants, wolves, etc. 4. A reel or skein ( anṭī ). Goṭe kī augī .
s. f. A whip like a waggonerʼs, about seven cubits long, used in training horses.

def: s.f. A pit or hole for catching elephants, tigers, &c. ( = obhī, q.v.).
adj. Without virtue or science, ignorant, worthless, vicious, depraved, corrupt; — s.m. A vicious or corrupt person.
more: see اوگي augun ) adj. Without virtue or science, ignorant.

def: s.m. Highest point, top, summit, vertex; zenith; height, altitude, ascendancy; highest apsis of a planet; highest position, rank, or dignity; preferment, promotion; prosperity
more: n. m. 1. The highest point; zenith; climax. 2. The highest post, rank, or dignity. 3. Prosperity; easy circumstances; competency ( auj mauj ). auj-i-falak; H. akkās-choṭī , n. m. The zenith. auj ko pauṅhchnā , v. n. To acquire the highest rank, dignity, power, etc.; to succeed or prosper greatly.
s. m. 1. Height, summit, the top. 2. Dignity, preferment, promotion. 3. The higher apsis of a planet.
subs: To attain to the summit of good fortune, &c.; to be very prosperous; to obtain the highest position, or dignity.

def: W. Slang. n. f. H. u up, jhāṛnā shake off. 1. A thrust; push; shove. 2. Blow; hit; kick (from a fighting cock). aujhaṛ mārnā , v. a. To strike with the shield. aujhaṛ honā , E. v. n. To worry; harass.
adj. Restless, uneasy ( = be-kal)
more: adj. (dakh.) Uneasy. اوکل بيکل aukal be-kal, Restless, uneasy.

def: adj. Untimely, unseasonable ( = a-samay); born before its time (a child), base-born (child).
s.f. Restlessness, uneasiness ( = be-kalī).
adj. Untimely, unseasonable ( = a-samay); born before its time (a child), base-born (child).
more: adj. (dakh.) Untimely, born before its time (a child: Harris ).

def: s.f. (pl. of waqt), Times, hours; circumstances, state, condition; means, resources, power, ability
more: s. f. (plur. of وقت) Times, hours, circumstances. اوقات بسري auḳāt-basarī, s. f. Pastime, amusement, employment, livelihood. اوقات بسري کرنا auḳāt basarī karnā, To pass (oneʼs time).
subs: (-se), To pass (one's) days (in), to subsist (upon, or by means of), to earn a livelihood, to live
s.f. Passing (one's) time, occupation, employment, livelihood, means of living
auqāt-basarī kāṭnā = auqāt basar karnā, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَوْقَات (ʔawqāt), the plural of وَقْت (waqt), whence Urdu وَقْت (vaqt, “time”). Although this is the formal plural of vaqt, it is generally used as a singular feminine noun with a different sense from "time".

def: n. f. 1. Employment, or occupation of time. 2. Livelihood; means of living; source of income.
s.f. = aushadhi, q.v.
more: n. f. S. औषधि Medicine ( davā ); remedy ( ilāj ). 1. Chāo ghaṭe ghar nit ke jāë, bhāo ghaṭe kuchh mukh ke māṅge, Rog ghaṭe kuchh aukhad khāë, gyān ghaṭe kūsaṅ- gat pāë . Kabīr. From constant visits liking wanes, from asking oft, esteem, From medicine, disease, 'mong bad men judgement goes, I deem. 2. Bikh kā aukhad bikh . Prov. poison cures poison.
